concerns.text: FR-005 requires a sensitivity analysis to report 'significance rates' (count/percentage of significant lags) across the sweep. T035 only mentions 'reporting significance rates' in the description but lacks a specific task to calculate and output this rate as a distinct deliverable, risking the omission of the specific metric required by FR-005.

Spec Edge Cases explicitly mandate: 'What happens if the time-series length is too short for Granger causality (minimum N < 20)? (Script must exit with error...)'. T023 only handles gaps > 3 days. There is no task to implement the N < 20 check and the specific error exit for the Granger test, leaving a critical spec requirement unimplemented.

T036 references 'SC-002 (target < 0.0167)' and mentions 'Holm-Bonferroni'. While the plan uses Holm-Bonferroni, the spec SC-002 explicitly defines the criterion as 'standard alpha level corrected... (Bonferroni)'. The task should clarify if it implements the spec's Bonferroni or the plan's Holm-Bonferroni to avoid ambiguity, as the spec defines the success criterion.
